calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hearings On The Merits Of Petitioners' Petition For A Writ Of Administrative Mandamus;
Off Calendar Jul-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Christopher A. DeWys, Esq. of the Law Offices of Christopher A. DeWys, Tel. 415.378.7533, for petitioner and moving party;
Gregory Tuss, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 510.622.2143, for respondent and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court ruled as follows: PETITIONERS SMOG TECH, HARJIT SINGH and PAUL VIRIYAPUNT'S Petition For A Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is taken under submission. The stay is extended until the entry of the Court's written statement of decision on the merits of the petition or to some future date as the Court may deem necessary. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Ntc Of Hearings On The Merits Of Petitioners' Petition For A Writ Of Administrative Mandamus;
Off Calendar May-16-2013 Continued To May-30-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHRISTOPHER A. DEWYS, TEL. NO. 392-2886 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONERS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONERs SMOG TECH, HARJIT SINGH and PAUL VIRIYAPUNT Hearings On The Merits Of Petitioners' Petition For A Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is continued on the Court's own motion to May 30, 2013. The stay of the decision to revoke Petitioners' licenses which the Court previously issued remains in effect through May 30, 2013. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)

Ntc Of Hearings On The Merits Of Petitioners' Petition For A Writ Of Administrative Mandamus;
Off Calendar Mar-20-2013 Advanced From Apr-17-13 To Apr-12-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Ntc Of Hearings On The Merits Of Petitioners' Petition For A Writ Of Administrative Mandamus;
Off Calendar Apr-12-2013 Continued To May-16-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CHRISTOPHER A. DEWYS, ESQ., 415.392.2886, FOR PETITIONER SMOG TECH.
GREGORY TUSS, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, 510.622.2143, FOR RESPONDENT BUREAU AUTOMOTIVE REPAIR.

RULING: no argument. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONERS SMOG TECH, A CALIFORNIA COMPANY, HARJIT SINGH AND PAUL VIRIYAPUNT'S Hearing On The Merits Of Petitioners' Petition For A Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is continued to May 16, 2013 to give the moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Petitioner must deliver a courtesy copy its opening memorandum of points and authorities that was filed on February 4, 2013 to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date no later than April 24, 2013 at 4:00 pm. The Court has copies of all of the petitioners' and respondents' other papers. The stay of the decision to revoke Petitioners' licenses which the Court previously issued remains in effect through May 16, 2013. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Ntc Of Hearings On The Merits Of Petitioners' Petition For A Writ Of Administrative Mandamus;
Off Calendar Mar-14-2013 Continued To Apr-17-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Christopher A. DeWys, Esq. of Automotive Defense Specialists, Tel. 415.392.2886, for petitioners;
Gregory Tuss, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 510.622.2143, for respondent.

RULING: The matter was discussed. The court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONERs SMOG TECH, HARJIT SINGH & PAUL VIRIYAPUNT's Hearings On The Merits Of Petitioners' Petition For A Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is continued on the court's own motion to April 17, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. Respondent provided courtesy copies of its opposition to the court. Petitioner must provide courtesy copies of its reply papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. The stay of administrative action is further extended to April 18, 2013. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
